puts.description: Writes each object to stdout followed by a newline. Arrays are written one element per line.

print.description: Prints each object to stdout with no separator or newline.

p.description: Prints the inspect representation of each object to stdout, with newlines. Returns the objects.

pp.description: Pretty-prints each object using the PrettyPrint library.

gets.description: Reads and returns a line from stdin (or the next program file in ARGV).

raise.description: Raises an exception. With no arguments, re-raises the current exception.

rand.description: Returns a random number. With no argument, a Float between 0.0 and 1.0. With an integer, a random integer from 0 up to (but not including) that value.

sleep.description: Suspends execution for the given number of seconds (may be fractional).

lambda.description: Creates a Proc object with strict argument checking and return semantics.

proc.description: Creates a new Proc object from the given block.

Array.description: Converts arg to an Array: tries to_ary, then to_a, then wraps in an array.

Hash.description: Converts arg to a Hash. Returns {} for nil or [], calls to_hash otherwise.

Integer.description: Converts arg to an Integer using strict parsing (raises on invalid input).

Float.description: Converts arg to a Float using strict parsing.

String.description: Converts arg to a String by calling to_s.

attr_accessor.description: Defines getter and setter methods for each named attribute.

attr_reader.description: Defines getter methods for each named attribute.

attr_writer.description: Defines setter methods for each named attribute.

initialize.description: Called by new to initialise a newly created object. Override in subclasses to set instance variables.

inspect.description: Returns a string representation of the object suitable for debugging.

to_s.description: Returns a string representing the object.

to_i.description: Converts the object to an Integer.

to_f.description: Converts the object to a Float.

to_a.description: Converts the object to an Array.

each.description: Iterates over the collection, passing each element to the block.

map.description: Returns a new array formed by applying the block to every element.

select.description: Returns elements for which the block returns a truthy value.

reject.description: Returns elements for which the block returns a falsy value.

reduce.description: Reduces the collection to a single value by applying the block cumulatively.

inject.description: Alias for reduce. Combines elements with a binary operation.

flatten.description: Returns a new array that is a one-dimensional flattening of self (recursively).

compact.description: Returns a new array with all nil elements removed.

uniq.description: Returns a new array by removing duplicate values.

sort.description: Returns a sorted array. Without a block, uses the <=> operator.

sort_by.description: Sorts by the value the block returns for each element.

first.description: Returns the first element, or first n elements if n is given.

last.description: Returns the last element, or last n elements if n is given.

length.description: Returns the number of elements in the collection.

size.description: Alias for length. Returns the number of elements.

empty.description: Returns true if the collection contains no elements.

include.description: Returns true if obj is present in the collection.

push.description: Appends each object to the end of the array.

pop.description: Removes and returns the last element (or last n elements) of the array.

shift.description: Removes and returns the first element (or first n elements) of the array.

unshift.description: Prepends each object to the beginning of the array.

join.description: Returns a string created by converting each element to a string, separated by separator.

split.description: Splits the string into substrings based on the delimiter, returning an array.

strip.description: Returns a copy of the string with leading and trailing whitespace removed.

chomp.description: Returns a copy of the string with the trailing record separator removed.

chop.description: Returns a copy of the string with the last character removed.

upcase.description: Returns a copy of the string with all ASCII lowercase letters converted to uppercase.

downcase.description: Returns a copy of the string with all ASCII uppercase letters converted to lowercase.

gsub.description: Returns a copy of the string with all occurrences of pattern replaced.

sub.description: Returns a copy of the string with the first occurrence of pattern replaced.

match.description: Converts pattern to a Regexp and returns the first match as a MatchData, or nil.

frozen.description: Returns true if the object is frozen (immutable).

freeze.description: Prevents further modifications to the object. Raises FrozenError if modified.

dup.description: Returns a shallow copy of the object.

clone.description: Returns a copy of the object, preserving frozen state and singleton class.

respond_to.description: Returns true if the object responds to the given method name.

send.description: Invokes the method named by symbol, passing any additional args.

tap.description: Yields self to the block, then returns self. Useful for method chaining and debugging.

then.description: Yields self to the block, returning the block's return value.

times.description: Iterates n times, passing integers from 0 to n-1 to the block.

upto.description: Iterates from self up to limit, passing each integer to the block.

downto.description: Iterates from self down to limit, passing each integer to the block.

abs.description: Returns the absolute value of the number.

ceil.description: Returns the smallest number greater than or equal to self with precision ndigits.

floor.description: Returns the largest number less than or equal to self with precision ndigits.

round.description: Returns self rounded to ndigits decimal places.

keys.description: Returns all the keys of the hash as an array.

values.description: Returns all the values of the hash as an array.

fetch.description: Returns the value for the given key. Raises KeyError if not found and no default given.

merge.description: Returns a new hash that is the merge of self with each other hash.

each_with_object.description: Iterates over the collection, passing each element and the given object to the block.

each_with_index.description: Iterates over the collection, passing each element and its index to the block.

zip.description: Merges self with successive elements from each argument array.

min.description: Returns the minimum element of the collection.

max.description: Returns the maximum element of the collection.

count.description: Returns the count of items matching the block condition (or total count without a block).

any.description: Returns true if the block returns truthy for any element.

all.description: Returns true if the block returns truthy for all elements.

none.description: Returns true if the block returns falsy for all elements.

flat_map.description: Maps and flattens one level: returns a new array from block results concatenated together.

group_by.description: Groups elements by the return value of the block, returning a hash of arrays.

chunk.description: Enumerates elements, grouping consecutive elements with the same block return value.

take.description: Returns the first n elements of the collection.

drop.description: Returns all elements after the first n.

new.description: Allocates and initializes a new object of the class, calling initialize with args.

object_id.description: Returns an integer identifier for the object, unique for the duration of the program.

class.description: Returns the class of the object.

is_a.description: Returns true if the object is an instance of class or its subclass.

kind_of.description: Alias for is_a?. Returns true if the object is an instance of class or its subclass.

nil.description: Returns true only if the object is nil.

freeze.description: Makes the object immutable. Attempting to modify a frozen object raises FrozenError.
